session.name: RunPod Builder thin-base cu130 rebuild + local ComfyUI v0.25.1 reinstall + LTX-2.3 workflow scripts

goal.original: Test purchased LTX-2.3 (NerdyRodent) ComfyUI video workflow on RunPod using the Cubric Vision Builder Pod; produce a model-download script + node-install script; get the Builder image matching a clean, standard ComfyUI; reinstall the local authoring rig clean.
goal.summary: Builder image v0.1.3-cu130 built + pushed to GHCR (thin-base: zero nodes baked, scripts install nodes/models at runtime). Local rig reinstalled clean (ComfyUI v0.25.1, all nodes, RES4LYF pinned). On a real RTX 3090 Pod (driver 580, CUDA 13.0) install_nodes.sh ran clean. NEXT: run install_models_ltx23.sh on the Pod, load workflow, generate. Then fix the Builder's Jupyter (multi-terminal + upload broken).

completed: Read LTX-2.3 workflow JSON + NerdyRodent Patreon PDF; extracted full model list
Decided diffusion = fp8_scaled (NOT mxfp8 — Blackwell-only, won't run on 3090/4090)
Wrote install_models_ltx23.sh (11 weights ~45GB: diffusion, VAEs, gemma fp4, upscaler, IC-LoRA, SDPose, abliterated, 2x BFS; Ollama excluded)
Local rig: fresh ComfyUI v0.25.1 portable on G:\ComfyUi (cu130, py3.13, torch 2.12)
Local: installed triton + sage + cupy-cuda13x + imageio-ffmpeg + Manager + 8 nodes + RES4LYF (pinned 419de2d) + 4 detection weights
Local: YAML (checkpoints/diffusion_models/loras -> C:/AI) + launcher flags (--use-sage-attention --preview-method taesd --output-directory D:\WORK\Images\Outputs)
REDESIGNED Builder to thin-base: image bakes ZERO nodes (Manager only); install_nodes.sh + install_models_ltx23.sh ship in image at /opt
Builder switched cu128 -> cu130 (matches local; faster/cheaper tests), torch 2.12+cu130, ComfyUI pinned eca4757 (v0.25.1)
Built + pushed ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-builder:v0.1.3-cu130 (digest sha256:134c656...); fixed 2 build failures (torchaudio 2.12->2.11; sage needs TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST)
Verified on real 3090 Pod: boots (driver 580.126.20, CUDA 13.0), install_nodes.sh runs clean (cupy-cuda13x ok)
Docker cleanup: pruned 16->2 images + 42GB cache (vhdx compact still pending, blocked by VS Code Docker ext lock)
pending: Run /opt/install_models_ltx23.sh on the 3090 Pod (RunPod Web Terminal), ~45GB via aria2c
Restart ComfyUI (8188), load LTX-2.3_nerdyRodent.json, generate to validate the workflow

Fix Builder Jupyter: make image's own terminal-capable JupyterLab win port 8888 (edit start-builder.sh: kill RunPod base Jupyter, start ours) -> restores multi-terminal + drag-drop upload; rebuild image
User to bump RunPod template image tag -> v0.1.3-cu130 (USER-only console op)
MPI-117 node-lock system + MPI-118 app ComfyUI bump to v0.25.1 (separate cards, RunPod branch only)

allowed_actions: rebuild + push Builder image (image build/publish authorized; NO live Pod create/delete/deploy)

completed: memory project_comfyui_portable_ships_cu130 (cu130 facts + 3090/580 boot data point)
memory project_builder_install_scripts (canonical script location + update procedure)
MPI-117 brief (node lock: per-node source Registry/tag/SHA; RES4LYF tagless; MpiNodes registry 1.1.1)
MPI-118 brief (app bump v0.25.1, cu130 guard) + MEMORY.md index pointers
pending: Confirm LTX-2.3 workflow actually generates on the Pod (validation not yet done)
Builder Jupyter fix not yet implemented (multi-terminal + upload still broken)
vhdx compact on D: still pending (run Optimize-VHD before opening VS Code, which holds the Docker lock)
Collect more RunPod host driver samples before deciding to move PRODUCT pod to cu130

next_action.description: On the running RTX 3090 Pod, in the RunPod Web Terminal run: cd /opt/ComfyUI && bash /opt/install_models_ltx23.sh (~45GB). Then restart ComfyUI (8188), load LTX-2.3_nerdyRodent.json, and generate to confirm the workflow runs. After that, implement the Builder Jupyter fix (own JupyterLab wins 8888) and rebuild.

key_decisions: Diffusion model = fp8_scaled NOT mxfp8 (mxfp8 = Blackwell sm_120 only; 3090/4090 can't run it)
Thin-base Builder: bake stable base (ComfyUI core + torch + sage + Manager), script everything volatile (nodes + weights) so node bumps DON'T need an image rebuild

Builder = cu130 (matches local rig, faster/cheaper tests); PRODUCT pod stays cu128 for host coverage. CUDA differing is fine — same ComfyUI version means workflows port; CUDA is NOT part of workflow parity
RES4LYF pinned to SHA 419de2d (tagless repo — SHA-pin is the only stable lock); local-only for now, future app dep via MPI-117
Node version lock SOURCE is per-node: Comfy Registry version (MpiNodes 1.1.1) / GitHub tag / GitHub SHA (RES4LYF) — see MPI-117
constraints: RunPod base Jupyter has DISABLED terminal launcher ('Launcher Error: Not Found') + flaky drag-drop upload -> use RunPod WEB TERMINAL, not Jupyter terminal. This is the multi-terminal + upload pain to fix.
Scripts are BAKED in the image at /opt/install_nodes.sh + /opt/install_models_ltx23.sh — no upload needed, just run them
cu130 image needs host driver >=580 to boot; verified a 3090 host had 580
Pod python is 3.12 (base default), local rig is 3.13 — harmless, but cupy/sage wheels resolve cp312 on Pod
Frame-Interp install.py's own cupy-wheel build FAILS (pkg_resources/no-GPU) — script overrides with cupy-cuda13x which SUCCEEDS; the traceback is expected + handled
torchaudio LAGS torch: valid cu130 trio = torch 2.12.0 / torchvision 0.27.0 / torchaudio 2.11.0 (NOT 2.12)
sage source build needs TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=8.6;8.9;12.0 (Docker has no GPU to probe)
Live Pod create/delete/deploy = USER-only; image build/push is authorized
All RunPod work lands on the RunPod branch ONLY, never master

resume_prompt: You are resuming work on the Cubric Vision RunPod Builder + LTX-2.3 workflow test (branch: RunPod). Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/d5350b4a-6902-4956-ac45-30cce8dcb496.json for full context, then read the three memory files listed in files_to_read_first. State: the Builder image v0.1.3-cu130 is built + pushed to GHCR (thin-base — bakes ZERO custom nodes; install_nodes.sh + install_models_ltx23.sh ship baked at /opt/ and install nodes/weights at Pod runtime). The local authoring rig (G:\ComfyUi) is freshly reinstalled (ComfyUI v0.25.1, cu130, all nodes incl. RES4LYF pinned 419de2d). On a live RTX 3090 Pod (driver 580, CUDA 13.0) install_nodes.sh ran clean. The NEXT action is: in the RunPod Web Terminal run `cd /opt/ComfyUI && bash /opt/install_models_ltx23.sh` (~45GB), then restart ComfyUI (8188), load LTX-2.3_nerdyRodent.json and generate to validate. After that, fix the Builder's Jupyter so its own terminal-capable JupyterLab wins port 8888 (edit start-builder.sh to kill the RunPod base Jupyter and start ours) — this restores multi-terminal + drag-drop upload, which RunPod's base Jupyter breaks (use the Web Terminal until fixed). Constraints: live Pod create/delete/deploy is USER-only (image build/push is fine); all work lands on the RunPod branch, never master; CUDA differing across environments is fine (workflows port by ComfyUI+node version, not CUDA). Related cards: MPI-117 (node version-lock system) and MPI-118 (app ComfyUI bump to v0.25.1) — do not start them unless asked.
